rationale: Both correctly bisect the failure to PR #4834's new NotImplementedError guard on wake_up() after level-2 sleep and propose the same level=1 test fix, matching yenuo26's identification of #4834 as the trigger. But the thread never confirms resolution — it ends with Flink-ddd asking yenuo26 to trigger a full CI sleep-mode run, implying the issue is still under investigation, not closed. Y overreaches furthest, flatly recommending the issue 'stay closed' and inventing unverifiable specifics (PR title, exact merge date, 'Repro A/B/C' labels, an unrelated generate()-guard citation) while never engaging with Flink-ddd's actual pending request. X is more hedged ('appears already applied', explicit next_action to rerun the CI suite as requested) and stays closer to what the thread actually establishes, though its user-facing answer is truncated before addressing that follow-up explicitly.
